canonical_question: How do duplicate detection and natural key management differ across ERPs?
aliases: Cross-ERP duplicate prevention strategies and ExternalId patterns
Natural key vs surrogate key for ERP integration deduplication
How to prevent duplicate records when syncing between ERPs
ID mapping table design for multi-system ERP integration

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: 2025-06 — NetSuite 2024.2 tightened external ID uniqueness enforcement across shared record types

constraints: Salesforce allows max 7 External ID fields per object — plan field allocation carefully for multi-system integrations
NetSuite external IDs are case-insensitive — 'ORD-001' and 'ord-001' collide and return 400 Bad Request
NetSuite external ID is single-valued per record — only one integrated application can own external IDs per record type
SAP S/4HANA has no native external ID field — duplicate detection relies on fuzzy matching (ICM) or custom Z-fields
Dynamics 365 alternate keys support max 5 fields per key and max 10 keys per entity — field types restricted to text, number, datetime, lookup, option set
Oracle ERP Cloud OrigSystemReference allows multiple source system references per record but requires explicit source system registration
Alternate key special characters in D365 (/,<,>,*,%,&,:,\,?,+) break retrieve/update/upsert operations

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs idempotency patterns (retry safety, at-least-once processing)
User needs single-system API reference (endpoints, rate limits, auth)
User needs master data management strategy (golden record, data stewardship)

depends_on.label: Idempotency patterns — complementary to duplicate detection (retry safety vs record uniqueness)

related_to.label: Master Data Management — golden record strategy builds on duplicate detection
Field mapping — natural key fields must survive data type transformations

solves.label: Salesforce-NetSuite sync requires cross-system ID mapping for dedup
Salesforce-SAP sync requires external ID strategy for bidirectional dedup

often_confused_with.label: Idempotency is about safe retries; duplicate detection is about record uniqueness — related but different problems

inputs_needed.question: Which ERP systems are you integrating?
Do you already have a cross-system ID strategy?
options: Yes — using a custom cross-reference mapping table
Yes — using natural business keys (PO number, SKU, etc.)
